key_drivers: Hawkish-hold Fed pricing and a firmer dollar kept broad financial conditions from turning outright supportive.
Slower GDPNow, tighter credit spreads, and lower rate volatility created a mixed but still constructive backdrop for selective equity and safe-haven exposures.
Risk-off volatility, stronger geopolitical tension in energy, and a supportive ETF-flow signal lifted gold and crude, while AUD was held back by risk sentiment and weaker copper.

fallbacks_used: ForexFactory calendar page was blocked, so the calendar section used the Federal Reserve events page and accessible weekly calendar coverage instead.
Some cross-market direction inputs were classified from accessible live page changes rather than downloadable exchange data feeds.

fed_stance.note: CME FedWatch showed the May 2026 FOMC meeting overwhelmingly priced as no change, with a small residual probability of a hike and no meaningful easing probability, which maps to a hawkish hold (-1).

real_yields.note: FRED DFII10 printed 1.95 on 2026-04-09 versus 1.96 on 2026-04-08 and 1.99 on 2026-04-03, so the move remained within the methodology's flat band (0).

usd_dxy.note: TradingView showed DXY higher on the day and back above the psychologically important 100 area, so the dollar factor is classified as strong/rising (-1).

risk_mood.note: Cboe VIX was above 20 during the accessed session, which classifies the regime as risk-off (+1) for the model.

growth_narrative.note: Atlanta Fed GDPNow was 1.3% on April 9 versus 1.6% on April 2, so the U.S. growth narrative is slowing (+1).

oil_supply_shock.note: Current energy reporting tied to the latest EIA week and broader market coverage still reflected tightening supply and disruption risk, so the crude supply factor remains positive (+1).

gold_etf_flows.note: The World Gold Council weekly market monitor described ETF demand as supportive and consistent with inflows, so the precious-metals flow factor is positive (+1).

ecb_stance.note: The March 19 ECB communication preserved a comparatively restrictive stance and did not signal an imminent dovish pivot, so the euro policy factor is treated as supportive (+1).

rate_diff_eur_usd.note: Because the ECB remained relatively firm while the Fed outlook stayed restrictive but unchanged, the EUR-USD rate differential signal was treated as broadly stable (0) rather than clearly widening.

rba_stance.note: The latest RBA decision kept policy tight and maintained a firm inflation-fighting posture, supporting a hawkish RBA classification (+1).

rate_diff_aud_usd.note: With the RBA cash rate still above the effective midpoint of the Fed target range, the AUD-USD rate differential remains supportive (+1).

usd_dxy.note: TradingView showed DXY higher on the day and back above the psychologically important 100 area, so the dollar factor is classified as strong/rising (-1).

risk_sentiment_aud.note: Given VIX above 20, the commodity-currency backdrop is risk-off rather than risk-on, so the AUD-specific sentiment factor is negative (-1).

china_growth.note: China's official March manufacturing PMI rose back above 50, indicating an accelerating China-growth proxy (+1).

copper.note: TradingEconomics showed copper weaker during the accessed session, so the industrial-metals signal is classified as falling (-1).
